overview.value: English Ivy is a trailing vine with lobed leaves. Indoors it prefers cool rooms, bright filtered light, and evenly moist soil.

careDifficultyDescription.value: Adaptable, but dislikes hot dry rooms and very dry soil.

light.value: Bright indirect light; variegated forms need more brightness.

water.value: Water when the top 2 cm of soil dries.

humidityDescription.value: Moderate humidity helps reduce crispy leaf edges.

diseaseRiskDescription.value: Spider mites are more likely in hot, dry indoor air.

fertilizer.value: Feed monthly during spring and summer.

tips.value: Trim vines regularly for a denser, less tangled plant.
